changes_made: Renamed k8s_admission_controller_vulnerability_scanning_configured to k8s_admission_controller_image_vulnerability_scan_enforced for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to vulnerability scanning of container images and the enforcement of these scans via admission controllers. The original names were mostly accurate, but one function name was adjusted for better clarity and consistency with Kubernetes terminology.
naming_improvements: Ensure that function names clearly specify the resource and action, such as 'image_vulnerability_scan' instead of just 'vulnerability_scanning'.
title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Discoverable Information
step1_reasoning: The control RA-5 (4) focuses on vulnerability monitoring and scanning, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment and that admission controllers enforce these scans. Kubernetes environments can integrate with image scanning tools to detect vulnerabilities in container images, and admission controllers can be configured to block deployment of images with known vulnerabilities.
